description: Two hooks implementing the generator/arbiter model economy. (1) SessionStart on startup/resume — injects a short reminder (session-start-economy.js) that the premium session model should spend its tokens on judgment while bulk reading/exploration and bulk generation are delegated to a cheaper tier; the reminder carries the absolute path to references/economy-playbook.md (passed as ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}) so the agent can read the full split on demand. Static context injection, writes nothing. (2) PreToolUse on the subagent-spawn tool (matcher Task|Agent) — economy-checkpoint.js inspects the spawn: when it targets a built-in bulk agent that inherits the session model (Explore / general-purpose, or an empty subagent_type which defaults to general-purpose) AND no model was pinned, it rewrites tool_input via updatedInput to add model:haiku for Explore (pure read-only lookup) or model:sonnet for general-purpose/empty (may involve multi-step work) — broad exploration is bulk work; the premium tier is reserved for judgment — and injects an additionalContext note telling the model it was auto-pinned and to re-issue with model:opus if the search needs cross-file reasoning. Custom agents (own model frontmatter) and already-pinned spawns are passed through untouched. It never denies and never sets permissionDecision, so it does not bypass the user's Task permission settings. Fail-open on any parse/shape error; reads stdin only, writes nothing.
